claims.claim: 17 Schools in Kelantan Flood Zones Damaged for 4th Consecutive Year
claims.source: NADMA Daily Situation Report, 26 November 2025, reports Kelantan had 14 primary schools, 2 secondary schools, 1 vocational college and 1 PERMATA affected by floods: https://mydims.nadma.gov.my/storage/Laporan%20Situasi%20Semasa/2025/Laporan%20Situasi%20Semasa%2026-11-2025.pdf
claims.correction: The closest verified current figure is 18 education institutions affected in Kelantan on 26 November 2025, of which 17 were schools/college-type institutions plus 1 PERMATA. The source says affected by floods, not necessarily damaged. I found no primary source verifying the same 17 schools were damaged for a fourth consecutive year.
claims.stale_check: The reconstructed analysis is dated 17 May 2026. The 17-school figure appears to align partly with a 26 November 2025 NADMA report, but the damage and fourth-consecutive-year framing remain unsupported.

claims.claim: Annual monsoon flooding damaged 17 schools in Kelantan for the fourth year running affecting 12,000 students.
claims.source: NADMA Daily Situation Report, 26 November 2025, reports 1,256 affected persons in Kelantan among pupils, teachers/AKP and officers, not 12,000 students: https://mydims.nadma.gov.my/storage/Laporan%20Situasi%20Semasa/2025/Laporan%20Situasi%20Semasa%2026-11-2025.pdf
claims.correction: Replace with: As of 26 November 2025, KPM reported 18 education institutions affected by floods in Kelantan and 1,256 education-sector persons affected in Kelantan. Do not state 12,000 students or fourth year running unless a primary source is found.
claims.stale_check: No later primary source found that makes the 12,000-student claim correct.

claims.claim: 17 Kelantan schools flooded for the 4th consecutive year disrupting 12,000 students
claims.source: NADMA Daily Situation Report, 26 November 2025, lines 213-230: https://mydims.nadma.gov.my/storage/Laporan%20Situasi%20Semasa/2025/Laporan%20Situasi%20Semasa%2026-11-2025.pdf
claims.correction: The verified 2025 figure is 18 Kelantan education institutions affected and 1,256 education-sector persons affected. The 12,000-student disruption number is not supported by the cited primary data.
claims.stale_check: No evidence of a recent update making 12,000 accurate.

claims.claim: Annual repair costs exceed RM15 million with no permanent flood mitigation
claims.source: NADMA article, 25 March 2025, says RM26.4 million was allocated to repair schools in Kedah, Kelantan, Terengganu, Sabah and Sarawak damaged by floods from December 2024 to January 2025: https://www.nadma.gov.my/bi/media-en/news/3332-kpm-segera-kerja-baik-pulih-sekolah-rosak-akibat-banjir
claims.correction: Do not state RM15 million annually for Kelantan schools unless a Kelantan-specific recurring repair-cost source is found. The verified figure found is RM26.4 million across five states for a specific Dec 2024-Jan 2025 repair allocation.
claims.stale_check: No primary source found showing a later annual Kelantan school repair cost exceeding RM15 million.

claims.claim: Average learning time lost per affected student is 28 school days annually
claims.source: No primary source found. KPM/NADMA sources reviewed report affected institutions, PPS use and PdPR measures, but not an average 28-day annual learning loss.
claims.correction: Remove the 28-day figure or cite a primary education-impact study or KPM dataset calculating learning days lost.
claims.stale_check: No recent primary source found confirming this figure.

claims.claim: Cumulative loss over 4 years equals nearly half an academic year per student
claims.source: No primary source found. This claim appears derived from the unverified 28-day annual figure.
claims.correction: Remove unless the 28-day annual learning-loss estimate and the four-year continuity are independently verified.
claims.stale_check: No recent primary source found confirming this cumulative estimate.

claims.claim: MOE proposed relocating 8 schools to higher ground in 2022 but funding was not approved
claims.source: No primary source found in MOE, NADMA or parliamentary search results for an MOE 2022 proposal to relocate 8 Kelantan schools to higher ground or for funding rejection.
claims.correction: Remove or replace with a sourced statement. If this was from a parliamentary answer or MOE development proposal, cite the exact document and date.
claims.stale_check: No later source found confirming that such funding was subsequently approved or rejected.

claims.claim: Relocation cost of RM120 million compares to RM60 million in recurring repairs
claims.source: No primary source found. NADMA, 25 March 2025, verifies RM26.4 million repair allocation across five states, not RM60 million recurring Kelantan school repairs: https://www.nadma.gov.my/bi/media-en/news/3332-kpm-segera-kerja-baik-pulih-sekolah-rosak-akibat-banjir
claims.correction: Remove RM120 million and RM60 million unless primary budget, tender, MOE or parliamentary records support them.
claims.stale_check: No recent primary source found confirming either amount.

claims.claim: Climate projections show Northeast monsoon intensity increasing 15% by 2035
claims.source: Peer-reviewed source: Wong et al., Climate Dynamics, 2022, projects for 2031-2050 under SSP5-8.5 a decrease in northeast monsoon precipitation over the east coast of Peninsular Malaysia by about 5-11%, while extreme precipitation rates generally increase over much of the peninsula: https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00382-022-06363-5
claims.correction: Do not state a blanket 15% Northeast monsoon intensity increase by 2035. A more accurate statement is that projections are mixed: seasonal northeast monsoon precipitation may decrease along the east coast, while extreme precipitation rates may increase in many areas under high-emissions scenarios.
claims.stale_check: No later official or peer-reviewed source found confirming a 15% Northeast monsoon intensity increase by 2035.

claims.claim: Flood frequency and severity will worsen without adaptation investment
claims.source: Malaysia NDC 3.0 to UNFCCC, 2025, identifies adaptation priorities for 2026-2035 including water resources and infrastructure; peer-reviewed projections show increased extreme precipitation rates under SSP5-8.5 but mixed seasonal rainfall signals: https://www.unfccc.int/sites/default/files/2025-10/Malaysia%20NDC%203.0%20to%20UNFCCC%202025%20final.pdf and https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00382-022-06363-5
claims.correction: Qualify the claim. Evidence supports adaptation needs and possible increases in extreme precipitation, but the analysis overstates certainty for flood frequency and severity at these specific Kelantan schools.
claims.stale_check: No recent source found that removes the uncertainty.

claims.claim: Rebuilding the same schools in the same flood zones every year is not resilience. It is denial.
claims.source: NADMA/Bernama report, 15 November 2024, says 91 schools in Kelantan were identified as flood-risk schools, but does not verify the same schools are rebuilt every year: https://www.nadma.gov.my/bi/media-en/news/1444-883-sekolah-di-seluruh-negara-berisiko-banjir-kp-pendidikan
claims.correction: The opinion can remain only if the factual premise is softened: Kelantan has flood-risk schools and some education institutions are repeatedly affected, but the same-schools-every-year claim needs evidence.
claims.stale_check: No later primary source found proving the same schools are rebuilt annually.

claims.claim: Annual rebuilding in known flood zones substitutes repetitive repair for permanent relocation or mitigation
claims.source: NADMA/Bernama report, 15 November 2024, verifies 91 Kelantan schools identified as at risk of floods; NADMA, 25 March 2025, verifies repairs were funded across five states. Neither verifies annual rebuilding or lack of mitigation: https://www.nadma.gov.my/bi/media-en/news/1444-883-sekolah-di-seluruh-negara-berisiko-banjir-kp-pendidikan and https://www.nadma.gov.my/bi/media-en/news/3332-kpm-segera-kerja-baik-pulih-sekolah-rosak-akibat-banjir
claims.correction: State only what is verified: Kelantan has identified flood-risk schools and repair allocations exist. Do not assert annual rebuilding or substitution for relocation without project and budget records.
claims.stale_check: No later primary source found confirming the stronger framing.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: 17 Schools in Kelantan Flood Zones Damaged for 4th Consecutive Year
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The primary source closest to the 17 figure reports education institutions affected by floods, not damaged, and does not verify fourth-consecutive-year damage to the same schools.
No primary source found for 12,000 students. NADMA's 26 November 2025 report gives 1,256 persons for Kelantan among pupils, teachers/AKP and officers.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Annual repair costs exceed RM15 million
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The verified repair allocation found is RM26.4 million across Kedah, Kelantan, Terengganu, Sabah and Sarawak for Dec 2024-Jan 2025 damage, not an annual Kelantan-specific cost exceeding RM15 million.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Average learning time lost per affected student is 28 school days annually
cardinal_sin_flags.why: No primary source found for the 28-day learning-loss statistic.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: MOE proposed relocating 8 schools to higher ground in 2022 but funding was not approved
cardinal_sin_flags.why: No primary source found for the proposal, the number of schools, the 2022 date or the funding rejection.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Relocation cost of RM120 million compares to RM60 million in recurring repairs
cardinal_sin_flags.why: No primary source found for either cost figure in this Kelantan school-flood context.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Climate projections show Northeast monsoon intensity increasing 15% by 2035
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Peer-reviewed evidence found does not support this blanket claim; one relevant study projects decreased northeast monsoon seasonal precipitation along the east coast for 2031-2050 while extreme precipitation rates may increase.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Rebuilding the same schools in the same flood zones every year is not resilience. It is denial.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The rhetorical point depends on an unverified premise that the same schools are rebuilt every year in the same flood zones.

omitted_facts: As of 26 November 2025, KPM reported 73 education institutions affected by floods nationally, including in Kelantan 14 primary schools, 2 secondary schools, 1 vocational college and 1 PERMATA; Kelantan had 1,256 affected education-sector persons. Source: NADMA Daily Situation Report, 26 November 2025, https://mydims.nadma.gov.my/storage/Laporan%20Situasi%20Semasa/2025/Laporan%20Situasi%20Semasa%2026-11-2025.pdf

As of 4 December 2024, KPM reported 105 schools affected by floods nationally, including 67 premises in Kelantan, and 6,637 pupils/teachers/officers in Kelantan still at school-premise PPS. Source: NADMA Daily Disaster Report #07, 4 December 2024, https://www.nadma.gov.my/images/2024/File/SITREP/MTL2024/SITREP_07_-_4_Disember_2024_RALAT.pdf

As of 1 December 2024, KPM reported 159 schools affected nationally, including 80 in Kelantan, and 13,461 pupils/teachers/officers in Kelantan at school-premise PPS. Source: NADMA Daily Situation Report, 1 December 2024, https://www.nadma.gov.my/images/2024/File/SITREP/MTL2024/SITREP_04_-_1_Disember_2024.pdf

KPM identified 883 schools nationwide at flood risk before the 2024/2025 northeast monsoon, including 91 in Kelantan. Source: NADMA/Bernama, 15 November 2024, https://www.nadma.gov.my/bi/media-en/news/1444-883-sekolah-di-seluruh-negara-berisiko-banjir-kp-pendidikan

KPM said RM26.4 million had been allocated to repair schools in Kedah, Kelantan, Terengganu, Sabah and Sarawak damaged by floods from December 2024 to January 2025. Source: NADMA, 25 March 2025, https://www.nadma.gov.my/bi/media-en/news/3332-kpm-segera-kerja-baik-pulih-sekolah-rosak-akibat-banjir

A relevant peer-reviewed climate study projects decreased northeast monsoon seasonal precipitation along the east coast of Peninsular Malaysia for 2031-2050 under SSP5-8.5, while extreme precipitation rates generally increase over much of the peninsula. Source: Wong et al., Climate Dynamics, 2022, https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00382-022-06363-5
source_assessment: The original analysis cites zero sources, so source diversity is effectively absent. The claims require KPM, NADMA, JKR, parliamentary budget records, state education data and peer-reviewed climate literature, but none are cited. The strongest available primary/near-primary sources are NADMA situation reports quoting KPM data and official KPM/NADMA statements. Political, community, school-level and budget-document perspectives are missing entirely.
overall_assessment: The analysis is not reliable as published. It appears to blend a partly real 2025 Kelantan affected-institution count with unsupported or wrong claims about damage, 12,000 students, recurring RM15 million repairs, 28 days of lost learning, an 8-school relocation proposal, RM120 million relocation cost and a 15% monsoon-intensity projection. Several claims may be directionally plausible, but the specific numbers and causal framing are not supported by primary sources found.
